synthesis_text: Simple ways to reduce the risk of getting COVID-19 include practicing good personal hygiene, such as regular hand washing with soap and water, wearing a face mask in public and when in close contact with others, maintaining physical or social distancing, ensuring proper ventilation of indoor spaces, staying home if diagnosed with COVID-19 or if you believe you may be infected, covering coughs and sneezes with a tissue or your elbow, avoiding sharing personal household items, staying up to date on COVID-19 vaccinations, keeping unwashed hands away from your face, and quarantining if exposed to someone with COVID-19 [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006][doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00094][doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00119].

To reduce the risk of getting COVID-19, it is important to stay up to date on your COVID-19 vaccine to prevent serious illness [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]. Ensuring good indoor airflow can help prevent the spread of the virus [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]. Practicing physical distancing is also recommended to reduce the risk of transmission [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]. Wearing a mask in appropriate settings can protect yourself and others [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]. Regular handwashing with soap and water for at least 20 seconds is crucial [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. Using an alcohol-based hand sanitizer with at least 60% alcohol when needed is advised [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. Covering your coughs and sneezes with a tissue or your elbow is important [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. Avoiding close contact with anyone who is sick or has symptoms is recommended [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. Staying home if you feel ill or have COVID-19 symptoms is essential [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00022]. Finally, testing for COVID-19 if you have symptoms or after exposure is advised [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023].

To reduce the risk of getting COVID-19, it is important to avoid crowded places and practice physical distancing of at least 1 metre from others, wear a mask in public places where community transmission is occurring, wash your hands frequently with soap and water or an alcohol-based hand rub, keep indoor spaces well ventilated, seek medical care early if experiencing symptoms of COVID-19, stay informed about local COVID-19 guidance and updates from health authorities, and get vaccinated to help reduce the spread of the virus. Additionally, avoid going to public places if you or your child has symptoms, regularly clean and disinfect surfaces you frequently touch, quarantine if exposed to someone with COVID-19, and stay home if diagnosed with COVID-19 or if you believe you may be infected. Testing for COVID-19 is also recommended if you have symptoms or after exposure, and staying up to date on your COVID-19 vaccine can help prevent serious illness.
